questions.text: The Battle of Adwa (1896) was a significant victory for Ethiopia against which colonial power?
questions.explanation: On March 1, 1896, Ethiopian forces under Emperor Menelik II defeated the Italian army at the Battle of Adwa. It was the first major African victory over a European colonial power and secured Ethiopian independence.

questions.text: Which Ethiopian emperor is credited with modernizing Ethiopia and founding Addis Ababa as the capital?
questions.explanation: Emperor Menelik II (r. 1889-1913) founded Addis Ababa as the capital around 1886, modernized the army, introduced telegraph and telephone systems, and expanded Ethiopian territory.

questions.text: The Organization of African Unity (OAU), which later became the African Union (AU), was founded in which city?
questions.explanation: The OAU was founded on May 25, 1963 in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. It was transformed into the African Union (AU) in 2002. Ethiopia hosts the AU headquarters to this day.

questions.text: The Derg regime in Ethiopia came to power by overthrowing which ruler in 1974?
questions.explanation: The Derg, a military junta, overthrew Emperor Haile Selassie I in September 1974. The regime ruled Ethiopia under Mengistu Haile Mariam until 1991 when it was defeated by rebel forces.

questions.text: Which ancient African civilization built the pyramids along the Nile in present-day Sudan?
questions.explanation: The Kingdom of Kush (and later the Meroitic civilization) built hundreds of pyramids along the Nile in present-day Sudan between 700 BCE and 350 CE, influenced by but distinct from Egyptian pyramids.

questions.text: The Aksumite Empire is known for being one of the first states in the world to officially adopt which religion?
questions.explanation: The Aksumite Empire, under King Ezana, officially adopted Christianity around 330 CE -- making it one of the first states in the world to do so, even before the Roman Empire made it the state religion.

questions.text: The Scramble for Africa, during which European powers partitioned the African continent, mainly occurred during:
questions.explanation: The 'Scramble for Africa' occurred primarily between 1880 and 1900. It was formalized by the Berlin Conference (1884-85) at which European powers divided Africa without African participation.

questions.text: Which treaty following World War I created new nations and mandates in the Middle East and Africa?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Versailles (1919) ended WWI and reshaped global borders, including redistribution of German colonies in Africa and Ottoman territories in the Middle East as League of Nations mandates.
